fields.name: Wainwrights
fields.description: The 214 Lake District fells described by Alfred Wainwright.

fields.name: Welsh Nuttalls
fields.description: Welsh mountains over 2,000 feet with sufficient prominence.

fields.name: Peak District Summits
fields.description: Significant hills and summits across the Peak District.

fields.name: Yorkshire Dales Summits
fields.description: Significant hills and summits across the Yorkshire Dales.

fields.name: Lake District
fields.description: England's largest mountain landscape, home to the Wainwright fells.

fields.name: Scotland
fields.description: Home to the Munros and many of the highest mountains in the UK.

fields.name: Wales
fields.description: A mountain-rich region including Snowdonia, the Carneddau, Glyderau and Brecon Beacons.

fields.name: Peak District
fields.description: Rolling gritstone edges, moorland plateaus and classic English hills.

fields.name: Yorkshire Dales
fields.description: Limestone valleys, high moorland and famous summits such as Ingleborough, Whernside and Pen-y-ghent.

fields.name: North Pennines
fields.description: High moorland country including Cross Fell.

fields.name: Eastern Fells
fields.description: A major Wainwright group including Helvellyn, Fairfield and High Street.

fields.name: Far Eastern Fells
fields.description: Remote eastern Lakeland fells stretching towards Haweswater and Kentmere.

fields.name: Central Fells
fields.description: Central Lakeland fells around Langdale, Grasmere and Borrowdale.

fields.name: Southern Fells
fields.description: The high rugged heart of the Lakes, including Scafell Pike and Great Gable.

fields.name: Northern Fells
fields.description: Northern Lakeland fells including Skiddaw and Blencathra.

fields.name: North Western Fells
fields.description: A varied group of fells west of Keswick and Buttermere.

fields.name: Western Fells
fields.description: A dramatic western group including Pillar, Red Pike and the Buttermere fells.

fields.name: Scafell Pike
fields.summary: The highest mountain in England and one of the most iconic Lake District objectives.

fields.name: Helvellyn
fields.summary: A famous Lake District summit known for Striding Edge and Swirral Edge.

fields.name: Ben Nevis
fields.summary: The highest mountain in the UK and a flagship Munro.

fields.name: Snowdon / Yr Wyddfa
fields.summary: The highest mountain in Wales and one of the most visited summits in the UK.

fields.name: Kinder Scout
fields.summary: The highest point in the Peak District and a landmark moorland plateau.

fields.name: Cross Fell
fields.summary: The highest point in the Pennines and a major standalone summit.
